heading: Reimbursements
label: 2205 Reimbursements
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)22055:172g.; added (23d par.), .July 26, 1947, ch. 343, § 408Aug. 10, 1949, ch. 412, § 1163 Stat. 590is restated to reflect more clearly its purpose to authorize the Department of Defense to operate as an integrated department by permitting supplies to be furnished and services to be rendered within and among agencies of the Department of Defense and provide that reimbursements therefor be credited to authorized accounts and be available for the same purpose and period as the accounts so credited. (See Senate Report No. 366, 81st Congress, pp. 23, 24.)5 U.S.C. 172g

children.heading: .—Availability of Reimbursements
children.label: a .—Availability of Reimbursements
children.text: Reimbursements made to appropriations of the Department of Defense or a department or agency thereof under sections 1535 and 1536 of title 31, or other amounts paid by or on behalf of a department or agency of the Department of Defense to another department or agency of the Department of Defense, or by or on behalf of personnel of any department or organization, for services rendered or supplies furnished, may be credited to authorized accounts. Funds so credited are available for obligation for the same period as the funds in the account so credited. Such an account shall be accounted for as one fund on the books of the Department of the Treasury.

children.heading: .—Fixed Rate for Reimbursement for Certain Services
children.label: b .—Fixed Rate for Reimbursement for Certain Services
children.text: The Secretary of Defense and the Secretaries of the military departments may charge a fixed rate for reimbursement of the costs of providing planning, supervision, administrative, or overhead services incident to any construction, maintenance, or repair project to real property or for providing facility services, irrespective of the appropriation financing the project or facility services.
